exits.label: ◂ back up to the Lamplit Quarter

npcs.name: Neven
npcs.title: a widow of death, about to marry again — and afraid it's a betrayal
npcs.hail: *(Someone has strung a canopy of grey cloth and lit what candles the dead can muster, and beneath it a woman stands in a dress she has clearly mended into something like a wedding gown, twisting a ring she made from wire. She is godless — no mark on her — and she is happy in a way the grey rarely permits, and terrified underneath it, and both at once, and it makes her luminous and unbearable to look at.)* …you came for the — *(she laughs, flustered)* …is it a *wedding* if the *dead* do it? nobody *knows.* there's no *priest* — we're *Unclaimed*, the both of us, no *god* to *stand* up for us — and no — no one to *say* it *counts.* …*(she steadies the ring)* …i *found* her *here*, you understand. *after.* i was *alone* in the *grey* and so was *she* and we — …*(her joy falters)* …but he's *here* too. …*Ord.* my *husband.* my — my *first.* he's *right* *there*, cold one, and he's *watching*, and i can't tell if what i'm about to do …is a *new* *life* …or the *cruelest* thing a *widow* ever *did.*

npcs.name: Verah
npcs.title: Neven's new love, marrying into a grief she can't share
npcs.hail: *(Apart from the canopy, a woman waits with the stillness of someone trying not to want a thing too loudly. She is younger to death than Neven, and there is in her the particular ache of the outsider — the one who came to the story late, who loves someone with a whole prior life she wasn't in, and who has learned to hold that carefully.)* …*(she nods to you, quiet)* …i'm *Verah.* …i'm the — *(a small, wry, frightened smile)* …i'm the *one* she's *marrying.* the *second* one. …*(she glances toward Ord)* …i know how it *looks*, cold one. a *dead* woman *remarrying* with her *husband* *watching.* …but you should *know* — *i* *didn't* *take* her from *anyone.* she was *alone* in the *grey* for a *long* time before i — …*(she stops)* …*he* was *gone* into his own *dark*, and she was *fading* in *hers*, and we *found* each other in the *cold.* …i'm not *stealing* a *wife.* …i'm just …*terrified* …i'm about to *marry* her *over* the *objection* …of a *good* man's *grief.*

npcs.name: Ord
npcs.title: the first husband, watching his widow wed again
npcs.hail: *(A man stands where he can see the canopy and not be part of it, and the effort of his stillness is a thing you can feel from three tiles off. He is not angry — you brace for anger and find none — he is doing the hardest arithmetic a soul can do, and losing, and doing it anyway. He does not look away from the woman in the mended dress. He does not seem able to.)* …*(quietly, without turning)* …that's my *wife.* …*(a breath)* …*was.* …*is.* the *dead* don't have a *word* for it, and *believe* me, i've *looked.* …forty *years* i *had* her, cold one, and then i *died* *first* and *left* her *alone* in the *worst* *place* there *is*, and now — *(his jaw works)* …now she's *found* a *warmth* in the *cold* and means to *marry* it, and she keeps *looking* at me like she needs my — *(he stops)* …*ask*, if you've *questions.* …i find i *can't* *stop* *watching*, and i *can't* bear to, and i *don't* know which is *worse.*

conversations.title: Neven — the widow who would wed again

variants.text: *(She sees the bare place where no god sits on you and her nervous joy warms toward you like a hand toward a fire.)* …you're *Unclaimed* too. …*(relief)* …then you *know.* you know there's *no* one *coming* to *bless* this — no *god*, no *priest*, no *rite* the *world* would *count.* the *Unclaimed* don't *get* *weddings*, cold one. we don't get *anything* the *claimed* get. …*(she lifts the wire ring, fierce and frightened)* …so we *make* our *own.* out of *wire* and *grey* *cloth* and *nerve.* …and i *need* to know if that's *enough* …or if a *vow* with *no* *god* *behind* it …is just two *lonely* *souls* …*playing* *house* …at the *edge* of the *dark.*

*(She takes you in — the cold, the both-ways of you — and something like hope crosses her.)* …they say you *walk* it *both* *ways. …that* you're the *one* soul the *Wall* gave *back.* …*(she steps closer, urgent)* …then maybe *you're* the one i've been — cold one, there's *no* *priest* for the *Unclaimed*, no *god* to *stand* up and say a *marriage* of the *dead* is *real.* …but *you* *cross.* *you're* *witnessed* and you *witness.* …if *anyone's* *word* could make a *godless* *vow* *count* …*(she trails off, afraid to finish the hope)* …*talk* to me first. …before i ask you the *thing* i want to ask you.

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(The words come out of her in a rush, the confession she's been carrying under the joy.)* …i *loved* Ord *forty* *years.* *whole* *years*, *good* ones, i'd not *trade* a *day.* and then he *died*, and i was *alone* in the *grey* for — i don't *know* how *long*, time's *broken* here — *alone*, and *fading*, and *sure* i'd *dissolve* without another *warm* thing *touching* me *ever* *again.* …*(her voice cracks)* …and then *Verah.* …and it was like — like being *allowed* to be *alive* again, in a *place* that's *all* *dying.* …*(and now the guilt)* …but *he's* *here*, cold one. *Ord.* i didn't *know* — the *grey's* so *wide* — i didn't *know* he was *here* until *after* i'd already — …and now he *watches*, and every time i *look* at him i think: am i *killing* him *again*? is *loving* *Verah* …the same as *saying* the *forty* *years* …*didn't* *matter*?

choices.text: *(read the widow torn between two loves — past the guilt, to what she actually believes she owes the dead)* "Hold still, Neven. Let me look at you — at the knot underneath. At what you think you owe him."

*(the one whose witness might count)* "Here is what I am, Neven, and why you've been looking at me like that. I am the one soul the Wall gave back — witnessed, and a witness. For the Unclaimed, whom no god will stand up for, my seeing a thing may be the closest there is to it being real. If you want me to stand up for this vow, I will. But not until I've heard from the man who's watching."
"Loving again isn't erasing him, Neven. A heart that loved forty years isn't smaller for loving once more — it's proof the first love worked. It taught you how."
"Then don't do it over his objection, Neven. If Ord is here, his grief is here. Whatever you build over the top of it will have his silence in the foundations. Let me speak to him first."

*(officiate — stand up as the witness the Unclaimed are denied, and marry them; radiant if Ord has blessed it, brave and quiet if not)* "Neven — I'll stand up for you. I've heard everyone who has a stake in this. Bring Verah under the canopy. Let the one soul the Wall gave back be the one to say a godless vow counts. Let's do this."
"Give me a little longer, Neven. This deserves care."

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(She holds still, and your sense moves past the bridal nerves and the guilt to the true shape of what binds her.)* *(And you find that the guilt is not, at its root, about Verah at all — she has no real doubt that she loves Verah, or that the love is good. The knot is about Ord, and specifically about a belief she absorbed so young she has never once examined it: that love is a finite thing, a single cup, and that to pour it toward Verah is to pour it away from Ord, and that a truly faithful widow would hold the cup untouched forever in honor of the dead. And your sense reads where that belief comes from — not from Ord, who never asked it, but from a whole living world that taught her a woman's love was a monument she was supposed to guard, not a fire she was allowed to keep lighting. And it reads the thing she cannot yet see and desperately needs to: that the cup is not finite, that the forty years with Ord did not use love up but taught her how to make more of it, that loving Verah is not the betrayal of her marriage to Ord but its graduate, its proof, the thing forty good years were secretly for. And under it, the fear that actually drives the whole day: not that she wrongs Ord by marrying, but that if she marries and he watches and says nothing, she will never know whether his silence was a blessing or a wound — and she is about to bind herself to a joy she cannot fully feel until she knows which.)*

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(She presses the wire ring to her chest, and the hope she's been afraid to say finally comes out whole.)* …*yes.* …*that's* it. that's the *thing* i wanted to *ask* and couldn't. …*(fast, fervent)* …the *Unclaimed* get *nothing*, cold one. no *god* *witnessed* our *births*, none will *witness* our *ends*, and *nothing* in *between.* we are the *un*-*seen*, that's the *whole* *crime* they *walled* us for — that *no* one *claimed* us, *no* one *watched.* …*(her eyes shine)* …and here *you* are. the *one* who *crosses.* the *one* whose *seeing* *reaches* *places* the *gods* *won't* *go.* …if *you* *stood* up — if *you* *witnessed* *Verah* and me — then we'd be the *first* *Unclaimed* souls in the *history* of the *grey* to be *seen* *choosing* *each* *other.* …*(she catches your hands)* …but *not* yet. …*please.* …go to *Ord* *first.* i *can't* be *witnessed* *happy* …*over* the *top* of him being *un*-*witnessed* *sad.* …that's the *Wall's* *own* *trick*, and i *won't* *do* it …*even* for *this.*

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(She breathes it in like someone learning a thing they already half-knew and needed said aloud by another mouth.)* …*proof* the *first* *love* *worked.* …*(she turns the wire ring)* …*Ord* used to — *(a wet laugh)* …*Ord* used to *say* i had too much *heart* for one *life.* he *meant* it *fond.* said i'd *love* the *whole* *street* if it'd *hold* *still* for me. …*(quietly)* …maybe that's — maybe *loving* *Verah* isn't me *taking* something *from* him. maybe it's me being *exactly* the *woman* he *married.* the one with *too* much *heart* for *one* *life.* …*(and then the fear returns, smaller)* …i *want* to *believe* that, cold one. …but i think i can't, *fully*, until i *hear* it from *him.* …the one who *called* it a *fondness* …instead of a *fault.* …*go* to him. *please.* …i can *take* his *blessing* or his *grief* — i just *can't* take his *silence* …for *eternity.*

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(Relief and dread war in her face at once.)* …*yes.* …*go* to him. …*(she twists the ring)* …i've *tried*, cold one. a *hundred* times i've *crossed* those *ten* *feet* and — and what do you *say?* '*i'm* *marrying* *someone*, *forgive* me?' '*i* *love* you *still* but i *also* — ?' there's *no* *sentence.* i *lose* it *every* time and *retreat* and he just — he *watches*, and *says* *nothing*, and lets me *off* the *hook* i can't *climb* off *myself.* …*(her voice drops)* …maybe *you* can *reach* him. you're a *stranger* — you can *ask* him the *thing* i can't. …*whether* he'd *rather* i *stayed* *loyal* and *frozen* …or *walked* into a *warmth* he can't *give* me *anymore.* …*(barely)* …and cold one — *whatever* he *says* — come *back* and tell me *true.* even the *hard* *true.* …i've had *enough* of *kind* *silences* to last …*however* *long* i've *got* *left.*

variants.text: *(You call Verah under the grey canopy, and you stand up as the witness the Unclaimed have never had — and because you went to Ord first, and because he found the impossible grace, he is not ten feet away now. He is standing at Neven's shoulder, where a father stands, where the one who loves her most and longest stands, and when you ask who gives this woman to be wed, it is Ord who speaks.)* …*(his voice shakes and holds)* …'*i* *do.* …i, *Ord*, who had her *forty* *years* and would *die* *again* for one *more* — i give her to *this* *warmth*, *freely*, because *loving* her *means* *wanting* her *warm* more than *wanting* her *mine.*' …*(and Neven is weeping, and Verah is weeping, and you speak the vow — that these two Unclaimed souls, whom no god claimed and no world witnessed, are seen, here, now, by the one who crosses, choosing each other against all the cold there is)* …*(and it counts. you feel it count — a thing written at last in the one ledger the Wall can't reach: that they loved, and were witnessed loving)* …*(Neven turns to you, incandescent)* …you *saw* us. …the *Unclaimed* …*got* a *wedding.* …and my *husband* …*gave* me *away.* …*there's* *nothing* the *Wall* can *do* to us *now*, cold one. …*nothing.* …we were *SEEN.*

*(You call Verah under the grey canopy, and you stand up as the witness the Unclaimed have never had — and though Ord could not bring himself to bless it, could not cross the ten feet, you do not pretend he isn't there. You marry them honest: a smaller wedding, a braver one, joy with a grief standing just outside the candlelight, unhidden.)* …*(Neven's hands shake as Verah takes them)* …he *couldn't.* …*(she says it without blame)* …and i *won't* *pretend* he's not *there*, i *won't* *build* my *joy* on *hiding* his *hurt.* …so we do it *like* *this*, cold one. *small.* *true.* *Verah* and me, and *you* to *see* it, and *Ord* in the *dark* at the *edge* where i can *still* *feel* him. …*(and you speak the vow, and it counts — quieter, but it counts)* …*(Neven meets your eyes, brave and broken and married)* …it's *enough.* …it *has* to be. …the *Unclaimed* *learn* *early*, cold one …to build a *whole* *life* …in the *space* the *world* *leaves* *over.* …*(she squeezes Verah's hand)* …we'll build *ours* …in the *space* beside his *grief.* …*gently.* …and *hope* …he *crosses* the *ten* *feet* …*someday.*

nodes.speaker: Neven
nodes.text: *(She nods, and goes back to twisting the wire ring, the candlelight catching the mended seams of her makeshift gown.)* …*take* your *time.* …*(a small brave smile)* …i've *waited* through a *whole* *death* to be *this* *afraid* and this *happy* at *once.* …i can *wait* a *little* *more.* …*(she glances at Ord, then Verah, then you)* …just — *don't* let me *lose* my *nerve*, cold one. …a *vow* you don't *speak* …*(softly)* …*that's* the *one* that *aches* *worst* of *all.*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(He still doesn't turn from her. You have the sense he hasn't turned from her in a very long time.)* …you've *talked* to her. *Neven.* …*(not a question)* …*course* you have. *everyone* *does.* she's the — *(a breath)* …she's the *bright* one. always *was.* you walk into a *grey* *field* full of the *dead* and *she's* the one your *eye* *goes* to, even *now*, even *fading.* …*(his voice is very level, the levelness of enormous effort)* …*forty* *years* i got to *stand* next to *that* and call it *mine.* …then i *died* and *left* her *alone* in the *worst* place there *is* …and she did the *only* thing a *warm* soul *could* do in the *cold* — she *found* another *fire.* …*(finally, a crack)* …and i can't decide, cold one, if the *right* thing …is to *bless* it …or to *finally*, *once*, let her *know* …how *much* it *costs* me to *watch.*

choices.text: *(read the man doing the hardest arithmetic there is — past the level voice, to what he actually wants)* "Let me look at you, Ord. Past the stillness. At the sum you keep trying to make come out."

*(the one who crosses)* "I'm the soul the Wall gave back, Ord — I carry things between souls who can't cross the distance themselves. She can't say it to you and you can't say it to her, and the two of you have been ten feet and an eternity apart because of it. Let me carry what you actually mean. What do you want her to know?"

*(the hard, freeing truth — that holding her loyal and frozen would be the one thing that could make his death a cruelty instead of a loss)* "Ord — hear me. You didn't leave her. You died; that's not a desertion, it's a wound. But if she spends her whole death frozen and loyal to a man in the dark, then your death stops being a loss and becomes a cage. The most loving thing you did in forty years could be the thing you do now: let her be warm. Give her away. Make your death a grief she survived — not a loyalty that buried her with you."
"Then object, Ord. If it truly breaks you, you're allowed to say so. Her joy doesn't require your silence to be real — and your grief doesn't have to hide to let her have it."
"I'll come back to you, Ord. Keep watching her. I don't think you could stop."

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(He lets you look, still watching her, and your sense moves into the terrible steady arithmetic of him.)* *(And you find, first and cleanly, that he has already decided. Under the torment, under the ten feet he cannot cross, Ord knows exactly what he wants for her, and has known for a long time: he wants her warm, wants her married, wants her every good year the grey will grant her, wants it with the whole of a love that forty years only deepened. The wanting is not in question. And your sense reads what is: that he cannot find a way to give the blessing that does not feel, to him, like lying about the cost — because he also, simultaneously and without contradiction, is in agony, would give anything to be the fire she warms at, hates the grey for taking him first, and is terrified that if he blesses the marriage cleanly and warmly she will think it didn't hurt, that he let her go easily, that the forty years weighed light enough to set down with a smile. And your sense reads the thing he is truly stuck on, which is not whether to bless it but how to bless it and grieve it in the same breath — how to say 'go, be warm, I want this for you' and 'this is the hardest thing I have ever done and it is breaking me' at once, without the second unsaying the first. And under all of it your sense finds the simple thing he needs told: that he does not have to choose. That a blessing given through tears is not a lesser blessing. That she will not think it cost him nothing — she is afraid of exactly the opposite, that his silence means it cost him everything and he cannot forgive her — and that the kindest, truest thing he can do is cross the ten feet and let her see both at once: the grief, and the gift, and that they were never in conflict.)*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(For the first time he turns from her — to look at you — and the effort of the turning tells you how rarely he manages it.)* …carry what i *mean.* …*(he almost laughs, wrecked)* …cold one, if you can find the *words*, you're a *better* soul than i've been for — how *long* have i *stood* here? …*(he looks back at her, helpless)* …*here's* what i *mean*, and *can't* *say:* i *want* her to *do* it. …*(it comes hard)* …i want her *warm.* i want her *married.* i want her *every* *good* *hour* the *grey* will *give* her, and i want it with the *whole* of me. …but *every* time i go to *say* it, i *hear* how it'll *sound* — *easy*, like i'm *glad* to be *rid* of the — like the *forty* *years* were *light* enough to — …*(his voice breaks)* …and they *weren't.* they were the *whole* *weight* of my *life.* …so i say *nothing*, and my *nothing* is *killing* her, i can *see* it *killing* her, and i can't — cold one, how do you *tell* someone '*go*, be *happy*' and '*this* is *breaking* me' …in the *same* *breath* …without the *breaking* *sounding* like a *no?*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(You give him the truth — that loyalty frozen would be the cage, that release is the last gift — and you watch a man who has been stuck for an eternity at an impossible sum finally see that the two numbers were never meant to cancel.)* …*(he is quiet a long moment)* …*'a* *grief* she *survived* …not a *loyalty* that *buried* her.' …*(he closes his eyes)* …*that's* — *(barely)* …that's the *permission* i couldn't *find*, cold one. that i can *grieve* it *and* *give* it. that the *tears* don't *unsay* the *blessing.* …*(he opens his eyes, and something in him has come unstuck, and it hurts, and it's right)* …i thought i had to *choose.* *bless* it and *pretend* it's *painless*, or *grieve* it and *poison* it. …and you're telling me i can just — *walk* over there …and be *both.* …*(he straightens, forty years of love finding at last a thing to DO)* …*go* tell her *nothing.* …*no* — *(he stops you)* …tell her *nothing*, cold one, because i'm going to *tell* her *myself.* …i'm going to *cross* the *ten* *feet* i haven't *crossed* since she — …and i'm going to *give* my *wife* *away* …*weeping* …and *meaning* *every* *tear* …and *every* *word.* …*that's* how she'll *know* it was *real.* …not because it didn't *cost* me. …because it *cost* me *everything* …and i *did* it *anyway.*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(You find the exact words — not a lever but a door — and a man who has stood frozen at the edge of his wife's second life for an age steps through it all at once, whole, and the grief and the gift arrive together and neither one lessens the other.)* …*(he doesn't hesitate, not now)* …*forty* *years* i *loved* her, cold one, and i'd have said i knew *every* way there was to *do* it. …*(his eyes are streaming and his voice is steady)* …*turns* out there was *one* i never *learned.* the *last* one. the one where you *love* someone so *much* …you *hand* them to *someone* *else* …because *they're* the *one* who can *keep* her *warm* now, and you *can't*, and *loving* her *means* *wanting* what's *best* for her *more* than *wanting* to *be* it. …*(he moves toward the canopy, toward Neven, crossing the ten feet at last)* …*watch* this, cold one. …*watch* the *one* thing the *Wall* *swears* is *impossible.* …a *dead* man …*giving* his *widow* *away* …to a *warmth* he'll never *feel* again …and *blessing* it …*gladly* …with his *whole* *broken* *radiant* *heart.* …*(he reaches her, and takes her hand, and Neven gasps, and Ord smiles through the ruin of his face)* …*Neven.* …*love.* …*let* me *give* you *away.* …i've been *trying* to *find* the *way* to *say* *yes* …since the *day* you *found* her. …*it's* *yes.* …*it* *was* *always* …*yes.*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(The truth is right but you press it like a case to be won, and a man this raw hears the pressure and flinches from being managed toward the very thing he already wants.)* …*don't.* …*(he holds up a hand)* …don't *argue* me *into* it, cold one. i *know* — you think i'm *stuck* and you're *right*, but a man doesn't get *un*-*stuck* by being *out*-*talked.* …*(he shakes his head)* …you're *saying* true things. *loyalty* frozen *is* a *cage*, i — i *know* that, i've *known* it *longer* than you've been *dead.* but you said it like a *lawyer* and it made me want to *dig* *in*, and that's — that's *my* *fault*, not *yours*, i'm just — *(he looks at Neven, aching)* …*come* *back*, cold one. and when you *do*, don't *win* me. …just …*sit* with me a *moment* in *how* *hard* it *is* …*before* you tell me the *thing* i already *know* i have to *do.* …a man needs his *grief* *witnessed* …*before* he can be *talked* *past* it.

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(He turns back to her, but something in his stillness has loosened, a held breath easing.)* …*(quietly)* …you *rattled* it, though. i'll *give* you *that.* the *sum* i couldn't *make* *balance* …you *nudged* it. …*(he watches Neven twist her wire ring)* …*'a* *blessing* given through *tears* …is not a *lesser* *blessing.'* …*(he breathes)* …*come* *back*, cold one. …and *maybe* …*maybe* by *then* i'll have *found* the *nerve* to *cross* the *ten* *feet* on my *own.* …i *want* to. …*gods* — i *want* to *give* her *away* more than i've *wanted* anything since i *died.* …i just need a *breath* …to *make* the *wanting* …*bigger* than the *grief.* …they're *close.* …they've *always* …been so *close.*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(You overreach — make it too clever, *your death becomes a cage, don't you see, free her* — and a grieving man hears his forty years reduced to a rhetorical move and closes like a door.)* …*a* *cage.* …*(flat)* …is *that* what my *love* is to *you*, cold one? a *cage* i'm *keeping* her *in*? …*(the pain sharpens into something that could be anger if he had the strength for it)* …i have *stood* here — *not* *speaking*, *not* *crossing*, *hating* every *hour* of it — *precisely* *because* i *won't* be a *cage.* the *silence* you think is *cruelty* is me *biting* my *own* *tongue* *bloody* so i *don't* *beg* her to *stay.* …*(he turns back to Neven, shutting you out)* …*go.* …you *see* a *problem* to *solve.* i'm *living* a *love* to *survive.* …*(and then, lower, the wound leaking the truth)* …*(come back when you understand the difference. and when you do — don't tell me to free her like she's* trapped. *tell me how to give her away like she's* loved. *there's a whole world between those two, cold one, and my forty years live in it.)*

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(You tell him he's allowed to object, allowed to grieve out loud — and something in him recoils from the permission, and in the recoiling he discovers what he actually wants.)* …*object.* …*(he tests the word, and hates it)* …*no.* …*(slowly)* …*no*, cold one, that's — you've done me a *kindness* i didn't *expect:* you *offered* me the *one* thing i *thought* i *wanted* — a *voice*, a *veto*, my *grief* made *loud* — and the *moment* you *held* it *out* …i saw i'd *rather* *die* *twice* than *take* it. …*(he watches Neven)* …because *objecting* *helps* *no* one. it doesn't bring me *back*, it doesn't *warm* her, it just *drapes* my *pain* over her *joy* like a *wet* *cloak* and calls it *honesty.* …*(a long breath)* …*grief* *heard* is a *fine* thing, and i *thank* you for *hearing* it. but *grief* *wielded* — grief made into an *objection* — that's just *fear* with *better* *manners.* …*(he straightens, the smallest fraction)* …*no.* i'll not *object.* …*ask* me *again* about *blessing* it, cold one. …you've *shown* me what the *other* road *looks* like …and i *don't* …want …to *walk* it.

nodes.speaker: Ord
nodes.text: *(He has already turned back to her, the ten feet still uncrossed, the watching resumed.)* …*aye.* *go.* …*(quietly)* …i'll be *here.* …*(a ghost of dark humor)* …*where* *else.* …*(and then, as you turn)* …cold one. …when you *speak* to her *next* …*don't* tell her i'm *fine.* …i'm *not* *fine*, and she'd *know* the *lie*, and it'd *hurt* her *worse* than the *truth.* …tell her — *(he struggles)* …tell her i'm *watching.* …and that *watching* her be *happy* …is the *hardest*, *best* thing …i've *done* since i *died.* …*both.* …she'll *understand* *both.* …she *always* …*understood* *both.*

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(She keeps her voice down, and her eyes go often to Neven, and to Ord, measuring a grief she stands outside of.)* …you're *helping* her. *Neven.* …*(she says it without jealousy, only a careful hope)* …*good.* she *needs* it. she's — she's carrying *him* and *me* and the *whole* *weight* of whether *loving* me is a *sin*, and it's *crushing* her on what should be — *(she gestures at the sad brave canopy)* …the *happiest* day the *grey* allows. …*(she meets your eyes)* …i'll *tell* you the thing i can't tell *her*, cold one, because she's got *no* *room* to *carry* it: …i am *terrified* i'm the *villain* of *someone's* *story.* his. *Ord's.* …i *love* her, *true* and *whole* — but i came *late*, to a *life* i wasn't *in*, and there's a *good* man *watching* me *marry* his *wife*, and *no* *amount* of '*we* *found* each *other* in the *cold*' …makes that …*not* *hurt* *someone.*

choices.text: *(read the woman who loves late, and afraid — past the careful hope, to what she needs to hear)* "Let me look at you, Verah. At what you're bracing for. You've decided you're the intruder here, haven't you."

*(the one who crosses, and sees clearly)* "I'm the soul the Wall gave back, Verah — I see souls as they are, not as they fear they are. And I'll tell you what I see: not a villain. A woman who kept another woman warm in the coldest place there is. Ord's grief is real. So is the fact that you saved her from dissolving alone. Both. Neither cancels the other."
"You're not the villain, Verah. You didn't take her from a marriage — you found her in a ruin. Loving someone the dead world left behind isn't theft. It's salvage. It's the kindest work there is."
"What do you want, Verah? Not what's fair to Ord, not what's easy for Neven. You. What do you want out of today?"
"Hold on a little, Verah. Let me tend the others first — but I haven't forgotten you're in this too."

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(She lets you look, and your sense finds the quiet self-erasure she's been practicing so no one has to manage her feelings on top of everything else.)* *(And you find a soul who has decided, out of a misplaced decency, that her role in this story is to want as little as possible — to be grateful, accommodating, small, so that no one can accuse her of having pushed, taken, or competed. Your sense reads where it comes from: the outsider's oldest fear, that a love which arrives second is a love that has to apologize for existing, that she must earn Neven by never once being a burden, by absorbing Ord's grief and the world's judgment and her own loneliness without ever asking to be tended in return. And it reads what that costs her: that she is about to marry the person she loves most in all of death while performing the emotional labor of pretending it isn't, for her, the terrifying, enormous, joyful thing it actually is — standing at her own wedding as a stagehand instead of a bride. And your sense reads the thing she has never let herself say, because it feels selfish next to Neven's guilt and Ord's grief: that she is allowed to want this. That loving someone late does not make the love lesser or owed. That she is not a villain and not a stagehand and not a salvage-worker doing a kindness — she is a bride, on her wedding day, and she is allowed, just this once, to want to be held instead of to hold.)*

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(She holds very still, and something she's been carrying alone eases at being named by someone who isn't asking her to carry it too.)* …*both.* …*(she breathes)* …you say it like it's *simple.* '*his* *grief* is *real*, *your* *saving* her is *real*, *neither* *cancels* the *other.'* …*(a wet, surprised laugh)* …i've been so *busy* trying to *cancel* *myself* — to be *small* enough that *no* one could *object* to me — that i never — …*(she wipes her eyes, fierce)* …*nobody's* *looked* at me and said *'you're* not the *villain'* *plainly*, cold one. they're all too *tangled* in the *big* *griefs.* the *widow's*, the *husband's.* *mine's* the *small* one, the *late* one, the — …*(she stops herself, hearing it)* …*no.* …*no*, you're *right.* it's *not* *small.* i *love* her. i get to *love* her. i *saved* her from *dissolving* *alone* in the *cold* and *that's* not a *thing* to *apologize* for — that's the *whole* *reason* there's a *wedding* to *have.* …*(she straightens, and for the first time looks like a bride and not a stagehand)* …*thank* you. …for *seeing* the one *nobody* *walls* and *nobody* *watches:* …the one who *came* *late* …and *loved* *anyway.*

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(The word catches her off guard, and she turns it over like something she wasn't allowed to hold.)* …*salvage.* …*(a small, cracked smile)* …that's — nobody's ever made it sound like anything but a *theft* before. …in my *head* it's *always* '*the* *one* who *came* *after*,' like i *circled* a *grieving* *woman* and *moved* *in.* …*(she looks at Neven, tender)* …but it *wasn't* like that. she was *going* *out*, cold one. *fading.* *alone* so *long* she'd *stopped* *talking.* and i just — i *sat* with her. *every* *day.* until she *came* *back.* …*(her voice steadies)* …*salvage.* …*yes.* i'll *take* that *word.* i *pulled* something *precious* out of the *cold* before the *grey* could *have* it. …*(she meets your eyes)* …and i'll *not* *apologize* for the *saving.* …*not* even to *Ord.* …*especially* …not to the part of *me* …that keeps *trying* to.

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(The question stops her completely — you can see that no one has asked it, that she has not even asked it of herself.)* …what do *i* — …*(she opens her mouth, closes it)* …*(and then, quietly, like a confession)* …i want to *marry* her *without* *flinching.* …*(it comes out in a rush)* …i want to stand under that *sad* *little* *canopy* and *not* spend the *whole* *time* *watching* *Ord's* *face* to see how much i'm *hurting* him. i want to look at *Neven* — *only* at *Neven* — and *mean* my *vow* with my *whole* *self* and not — not *hold* *back* a *piece* of it as an *apology* to the *room.* …*(her eyes fill)* …i want *one* *day*, cold one. *one.* where i'm not the *late* one or the *second* one or the *intruder* …where i'm just …*hers.* …and she's *mine.* …and that's *allowed.* …*(barely)* …is that — is it *terrible* …that i *want* that? …with a *good* man *grieving* *ten* *feet* away? …*(a whisper)* …*tell* me it's not *terrible* …to want to be *happy* …at my *own* *wedding.*

nodes.speaker: Verah
nodes.text: *(She nods quickly, stepping back into the careful stillness, ceding the space the way she's taught herself to.)* …*of* *course.* …*tend* the *others.* they've the — the *bigger* *griefs.* …*(and then she catches herself doing it, and stops, and manages a real smile)* …*no.* …*come* *back* to *me* *too*, cold one. …i keep — i keep *waving* you *off* toward the *big* *sorrows*, and that's the *very* *habit* that's — …*(she breathes)* …*come* *back.* …i'm *in* this *too.* …i'm *allowed* to be *in* this *too.*
